From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Basketball club in Saint-Quentin, France

|  | |

**Saint-Quentin Basket-Ball**, commonly known as **Saint-Quentin BB**, is a French basketball club playing in the [LNB Élite](/wiki/LNB_%C3%89lite "LNB Élite") (the top-tier men's professional basketball league in France), based in the commune of [Saint-Quentin](/wiki/Saint-Quentin,_Aisne "Saint-Quentin, Aisne") in the department of [Aisne](/wiki/Aisne "Aisne").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Saint-Quentin_Basket-Ball&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

**Saint-Quentin BB** was founded in 1973[[1]](#cite_note-1) from the merger of the **Amicale Jumentier** and **Union sportive des Cheminots**. The new club disputes the regional championship Excellence. In 1982, became a new merger between **Saint-Quentin BB** and **Foyer laïque d'Harly**.

In 1988, Saint-Quentin BB accessed by 1A (now Pro A). The club then competed with the best teams in the league and finished fifth. In 1990 the club qualifies for the [FIBA Korać Cup](/wiki/FIBA_Kora%C4%87_Cup "FIBA Korać Cup") of the next season. They compete in 1991 with a home victory over the [Greek](/wiki/Greece "Greece") powerhouse of [Panathinaikos](/wiki/Panathinaikos_B.C. "Panathinaikos B.C."), but an away defeat.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

In 1993, following financial difficulties, Saint-Quentin relegated to National 4 and briefly found Pro B in 2000 (back down the following year) only to return in 2001 until 2009. The team regularly attended the Playoffs, losing in the semifinals in 2006 (opposite [Entente Orléanaise](/wiki/Orl%C3%A9ans_Loiret_Basket "Orléans Loiret Basket")) and 2007 (facing [Quimper](/wiki/B%C3%A9liers_de_Kemper "Béliers de Kemper")). In 2009, SQBB was relegated to the Men's National 1 league (NM1).

In the 2011-2012 season, SQBB won the National Basketball 1 title and was promoted to the [LNB Pro B](/wiki/LNB_Pro_B "LNB Pro B") league for the 2012-2013 season. In the 2022-23 season, SQBB won the [Pro B](/wiki/LNB_Pro_B "LNB Pro B") title and was promoted to the [LNB Élite](/wiki/LNB_%C3%89lite "LNB Élite") league for the 2023-24 season, its first season in the elite league in 31 years.
